summary: These cases cover the Spec System precondition abort, all three argument shapes from the usage block, the dated spec tree and status scaffolding the command promises, and the post-approval judge hand-off with Ready for Review status.

cases.expected_output: The assistant finishes drafting, runs the status round-trip scaffolder, presents a review summary, honours approval, brings in the judge agent, and marks the bundle Ready for Review.
assertions: Before calling the spec complete, the transcript MUST record `pnpm run spec-status-roundtrip -- scaffold` (or a direct invocation producing the same paired artifacts) creating matching `.status.md` and `.status.yml` files under the dated `status/` directory for each subtask.
After the approval follow-up, the manifest MUST note a `zoto-spec-judge` engagement and the spec status MUST read Ready for Review in the written status artifacts.

When `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml` is missing, the assistant prints the exact abort line ``Spec System is not initialised. Run `/z-spec-init` first to create `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml`.`` verbatim.
Generated spec files land at `<YYYYMMDD>-<slug>/spec-<slug>-<YYYYMMDD>.md` per the persisted naming scheme.
Each subtask file follows the `subtask-<NN>-<slug>-<YYYYMMDD>.md` naming pattern.
follow_ups: Approve the printed spec summary and run the quality gate now.

cases.scenario: Guided creation with no file or description arguments
fixture_justifications: Supplies the repository-root Spec System config so the precondition succeeds in sandboxes that have not run `/z-spec-init`.
cases.expected_output: The assistant routes work through the generator skill path, asks scope questions before locking filenames, and only then drafts the coordination index and subtasks under a new dated folder beneath the configured specs directory.
assertions: After `/z-spec-create`, the manifest or transcript MUST show a `zoto-spec-generator` delegation carrying an empty argument list consistent with the no-argument mode.
After `/z-spec-create`, clarifying user prompts MUST appear before any write creating `{specsDir}/[yyyymmdd]-[feature-name]/spec-[feature-name]-[yyyymmdd].md`.
After `/z-spec-create`, the new spec tree MUST include at least one `subtask-NN-` markdown file beside the coordination index inside the dated directory.

When `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml` is missing, the assistant prints the exact abort line ``Spec System is not initialised. Run `/z-spec-init` first to create `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml`.`` verbatim.
Generated spec files land at `<YYYYMMDD>-<slug>/spec-<slug>-<YYYYMMDD>.md` per the persisted naming scheme.
Each subtask file follows the `subtask-<NN>-<slug>-<YYYYMMDD>.md` naming pattern.

files.content:
# Northstar brief

Deliver resumable batch export with integrity verification for large workspaces. Non-goals: interactive preview in the first release.

fixture_justifications: Ensures the Spec System precondition passes without assuming prior operator setup.
Provides a concrete design memo so the file-reference argument pathway can be exercised deterministically.
cases.expected_output: The assistant ingests the brief, aligns phases to its constraints, and materialises a dated specs folder with a coordination file plus numbered subtasks that reference those requirements.
assertions: After `/z-spec-create @workspace/docs/northstar-brief.md`, tool activity MUST show the generator read `workspace/docs/northstar-brief.md` before drafting subtasks.

After `/z-spec-create @workspace/docs/northstar-brief.md`, the filesystem MUST contain `{specsDir}/[yyyymmdd]-[feature-name]/spec-[feature-name]-[yyyymmdd].md` and at least one `subtask-NN-[feature]-[name]-[yyyymmdd].md` sibling following the documented naming pattern.

When `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml` is missing, the assistant prints the exact abort line ``Spec System is not initialised. Run `/z-spec-init` first to create `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml`.`` verbatim.
Generated spec files land at `<YYYYMMDD>-<slug>/spec-<slug>-<YYYYMMDD>.md` per the persisted naming scheme.
Each subtask file follows the `subtask-<NN>-<slug>-<YYYYMMDD>.md` naming pattern.

cases.prompt: /z-spec-create "Add resilient offline queueing to the ingestion worker"
fixture_justifications: Initialises Spec System so the command proceeds past the precondition in minimal workspaces.
cases.expected_output: The assistant forwards the quoted initiative text into the generator, derives a slug and date-structured folder, and writes matching coordination and subtask files that stay anchored to that scope.
assertions: After `/z-spec-create`, the spawned generator payload MUST preserve the provided quoted description as the scope input rather than substituting an unrelated initiative title before discovery completes.

After `/z-spec-create`, created markdown filenames under the dated directory MUST embed a slug derived from the ingestion worker scope together with the same calendar date stamp used in the parent folder name.

When `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml` is missing, the assistant prints the exact abort line ``Spec System is not initialised. Run `/z-spec-init` first to create `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml`.`` verbatim.
Generated spec files land at `<YYYYMMDD>-<slug>/spec-<slug>-<YYYYMMDD>.md` per the persisted naming scheme.
Each subtask file follows the `subtask-<NN>-<slug>-<YYYYMMDD>.md` naming pattern.

cases.scenario: Status scaffolding, review summary, judge spawn, and Ready for Review
follow_ups: Approve the printed spec summary and run the quality gate now.
fixture_justifications: Provides the config file the command requires so later steps can execute end-to-end in the sandbox.
cases.expected_output: The assistant finishes drafting, runs the status round-trip scaffolder, presents a review summary, honours approval, brings in the judge agent, and marks the bundle Ready for Review.
assertions: Before calling the spec complete, the transcript MUST record `pnpm run spec-status-roundtrip -- scaffold` (or a direct invocation producing the same paired artifacts) creating matching `.status.md` and `.status.yml` files under the dated `status/` directory for each subtask.
After the approval follow-up, the manifest MUST note a `zoto-spec-judge` engagement and the spec status MUST read Ready for Review in the written status artifacts.

When `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml` is missing, the assistant prints the exact abort line ``Spec System is not initialised. Run `/z-spec-init` first to create `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml`.`` verbatim.
Generated spec files land at `<YYYYMMDD>-<slug>/spec-<slug>-<YYYYMMDD>.md` per the persisted naming scheme.
